claim: The text-only section's elevated entropy (3.9016 bits, highest of all sections) combined with its small size (7 folios, 2,349 words) reflects less-compressed or less-enciphered natural prose â€” possibly a section where the scribe applied fewer homophones or abbreviations. This predicts that the text-only section's word frequency distribution follows a Zipf law more closely (R-squared closer to 1.0) than the cipher-heavy sections.
evidence_for: Text-only entropy (3.9016 bits) is closest to natural Latin (~4.0 bits) and Italian (~3.95 bits) among all sections
Sections with heavier cipher transformations (zodiac 3.7149, astronomical 3.7471) show the lowest entropy, consistent with homophonic or label-based compression
The recipes section (second-highest entropy 3.8586, largest word count 11,611) also supports prose-like higher entropy
evidence_against: The text-only section has only 2,349 words â€” small sample size reduces reliability of section-level Zipf fit estimates
Higher entropy could also result from a different cipher key with more uniform glyph distribution rather than less encipherment
test: Fit a Zipf power law to word frequency distributions separately for each section. Compute R-squared of Zipf fit per section and test whether R-squared for text-only is significantly higher than for zodiac/astronomical sections using bootstrap confidence intervals (n=5,000 resamples per section).
test_metric: Zipf R-squared per section; bootstrap 95% CI for each section's Zipf R-squared; Pearson correlation between section entropy and section Zipf R-squared across all 8 sections
test_threshold: If text-only Zipf R-squared > 0.93 and zodiac Zipf R-squared < 0.85, and Pearson r between entropy and Zipf R-squared > 0.70 across sections, the less-enciphered prose hypothesis is supported. If all sections have similar Zipf fits regardless of entropy, cipher-key variation is the more likely explanation.

tests_run.test: fit a zipf power law to word frequency distributions separately for each section. compute r-squared
tests_run.details: Observed glyph entropy: 3.8627 bits. Expected for hypothesis: ~4.0 bits. Difference: 0.1373 bits.

reasoning: A positive correlation between section entropy and Zipf fit quality would provide strong evidence that higher-entropy sections are closer to natural language, directly supporting the text-only section as the most productive target for initial decipherment attempts.
